title: CS 461 Privacy-Friendly Baby Tracking App PRD (12/4/24)
html:
TL;DR

  • OSU CS 461 capstone Product Requirements Document — Group 27 building a privacy friendly baby tracking app.
  • Team: Bryant Shitabata (shitabab), Jacob Cocheu (cocheuj), Sonny Box (boxso), Yahir Gonzalez (gonzayah).
  • Core thesis: local first storage with optional cloud backup — counter-positioning against incumbent baby trackers that route sensitive infant data through third-party clouds.
  • Stack: React Native cross-platform, AES-256 local encryption, Gemini API (free tier with paid-tier risk noted), Supabase-style backend implied.
  • Compliance scope: GDPR, COPPA, CCPA, HIPAA-aligned (not mandated). Feature set: sleep tracking, bathroom (diaper/potty/accidents), feeding, growth milestones, photo journal.

Notes

This is Yahir's final OSU capstone PRD — the same Software Engineering II / capstone class flagged in the August 2023 todo as the last-class-needed signup. Document is heavy with character-level OCR garble (every n comes through as m, every r as í, every v as :) — Google Doc was apparently a paste from a styled PDF.

The PRD echoes Yahir's broader privacy tradeoff instinct from his earlier biometric recognition research-reading — encryption-by-default, minimal data collection, opt-in cloud. The "local-first with opt-in cloud" architectural choice is the same operating philosophy he later applies to Rankett and IRL Lore (private-by-default journal). This is the first PRD in the corpus where Yahir is named on a four-person engineering team, and the privacy-stance is unmistakably his.

Technical surfaces named: encryption (AES-256), authentication service, API gateway, MFA, sync conflict resolution, offline-first logging. UI principles: 3-tap navigation, one-handed operation, dark mode for late-night feedings. Success metrics: ≥90% encrypted data, 0 non-anonymized data access, ≤5 daily crashes per 100 users, 4★+ rating.
